seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with DSPy + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with DSPy + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI Coding Assistants with DSPy enables programming LLMs through composable, optimizable modules rather than brittle prompting, streamlining repository-aware code generation and debugging. However, optimized modules can overfit to training data, produce plausible-but-incorrect logic, or compose unexpectedly, risking code errors, security vulnerabilities, and compliance issues. Vex provides runtime verification to catch these failures before they reach production.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with DSPy
architectureOverview.description:
The DSPy-powered AI Coding Assistant processes repository context through a `RepositoryContext` module that extracts relevant files and dependencies. A `CodeGenerator` module produces fixes or new features using chain-of-thought reasoning optimized over test cases, followed by a `Validator` module that executes code in a sandbox and checks assertions. Iterative debugging flows through a `Debugger` module that analyzes test failures and proposes refined solutions.

DSPy signatures define structured LM calls (e.g., `generate_code(repo_context, task_description -> generated_code)`), compiled into optimized programs. The full pipeline wraps these module `forward()` calls with Vex verification to ensure code correctness, security, and compliance before execution.

This modular approach allows teleprompters to optimize over execution traces, but introduces risks when modules compose unexpectedly or overfit to synthetic training data.

risks.name: Overfitted Code Generation
risks.description: DSPy optimization creates prompts that pass training tests but fail on real repo edge cases
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies generated code against dynamic test suites and repo-specific invariants

risks.name: Plausible Incorrect Logic
risks.description: Chain-of-thought produces confident but wrong algorithms (e.g., off-by-one errors in loops)
risks.mitigation: Runtime assertion checking and symbolic execution validation

risks.name: Security Injection Vectors
risks.description: Generated code contains eval(), system calls, or dependency downloads without validation
risks.mitigation: Static analysis + AST scanning for dangerous patterns before sandbox execution

risks.name: License/Dependency Violations
risks.description: Code suggests incompatible licenses or malicious dependencies
risks.mitigation: Dependency scanning and license compatibility checking

implementation.title: Repository Context Signature
implementation.description: DSPy signature to extract relevant files and build execution context from git repo
implementation.code:
import dspy

class RepositoryContext(dspy.Signature):
    '''Extract relevant files for coding task.'''
    repo_path: str = dspy.InputField()
    task_description: str = dspy.InputField()
    relevant_files: list[str] = dspy.OutputField()
    execution_context: str = dspy.OutputField()

repo_context = dspy.ChainOfThought(RepositoryContext)

# Usage
context = repo_context(repo_path='/path/to/repo', task_description='Fix login bug').to_dict()

implementation.title: Code Generator Module
implementation.description: Generates code fixes with test assertions using optimized CoT reasoning
implementation.code:
class CodeGenerator(dspy.Signature):
    '''Generate correct code with test assertions.'''
    context: str = dspy.InputField(desc='Repo files + task')
    tests: str = dspy.InputField(desc='Expected test cases')
    code: str = dspy.OutputField()
    test_assertions: str = dspy.OutputField()

generator = dspy.ChainOfThought(CodeGenerator)
program = dspy.ModuleList(generator, dspy.Predict('test_assertions'))
result = program(context=repo_context, tests='assert login_successful(user)')

# Compile with test cases
tp = dspy.teleprompt.BootstrapFewShot()
compiled_generator = tp.compile(program, trainset=train_examples)

implementation.title: Sandbox Validator
implementation.description: Executes generated code and validates assertions in isolated environment
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

class SandboxValidator(dspy.Signature):
    '''Execute code safely and validate assertions.'''
    code: str = dspy.InputField()
    assertions: str = dspy.InputField()
    passed: bool = dspy.OutputField()
    error: str = dspy.OutputField()

validator = SandboxValidator()

def safe_execute(code: str, assertions: str) -> dict:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        path = Path(tmpdir) / 'temp.py'
        path.write_text(code + '\n' + assertions)
        result = subprocess.run(['python', str(path)], 
                              capture_output=True, timeout=30)
        return {'passed': result.returncode == 0, 'error': result.stderr.decode()}

implementation.title: Iterative Debugger
implementation.description: Analyzes test failures and generates refined fixes
implementation.code:
class Debugger(dspy.Signature):
    '''Debug failing tests with targeted fixes.'''
    failing_code: str = dspy.InputField()
    error_message: str = dspy.InputField()
    test_expectations: str = dspy.OutputField()
    targeted_fix: str = dspy.OutputField()

debugger = dspy.ChainOfThought(Debugger)

# Iterative debugging loop
def debug_loop(initial_code, tests):
    current_code = initial_code
    for i in range(3):  # Max 3 iterations
        result = safe_execute(current_code, tests)
        if result['passed']:
            return current_code
        fix = debugger(
            failing_code=current_code,
            error_message=result['error']
        ).targeted_fix
        current_code += f'\n# Fix iteration {i+1}:\n{fix}'
    return current_code
